content: We can reframe tool definition design for function-calling autonomous agents by treating the tool description crafting process as an ICM curiosity-driven reward problem: the agent gets an intrinsic reward for discovering gaps between the LLM’s current understanding of available tools and untested tool combinations, and iteratively rewrites tool definitions to reduce this prediction error. This creates a self-improving cycle where the agent autonomously optimizes the most critical component of its own function calling system.
source_topics: tool use function calling autonomous agent
trigger_topic: tool use function calling autonomous agent
